actionable_feedback: Ensure the workbook exposes formulas in a parseable way: (a) enable environment parseability or (b) add a 'formulas' or 'calc' worksheet that contains formula text for derived cells, or (c) export derived calculation ranges as CSVs showing the formula text.

In the expert memo add explicit cell-level citations that map memo rows/values to spreadsheet cells (e.g., Evidence!A2, Sensitivity!C5). For each sensitivity number included in the memo, include the exact sheet and cell address used to compute it.

Canonicalize submission metadata/summary fields so there is a single authoritative score/passed indicator. Remove duplicated/conflicting fields (e.g., reconcile deterministic_precheck vs model grader outputs) and document which score is canonical.
Label claims in the memo as 'memo-derived' vs 'spreadsheet-verified' when the workbook could not be parsed; after attaching parseable formulas re-run preview and update labels accordingly.
If environment/library issues are expected, also attach a plain-text formula export (or annotated CSV) that an automated grader can parse even if openpyxl is unavailable.
artifact_specific_comments: {'artifact_ref': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': "Preview parse failed with error 'No module named openpyxl'. Provide formulas export or ensure the workbook is readable by standard preview tools."}

feedback_summary: Both required artifacts were produced and the expert memo contains appropriate reasoning and sensitivity numbers. However the spreadsheet could not be programmatically parsed in previews (openpyxl_unavailable), the memo lacks explicit cell-level citations to spreadsheet cells, and the submission record contains inconsistent score/pass fields across deterministic and model grading outputs. These issues prevent programmatic verification of formulas and provenance and drove a conservative semantic downgrade.

recommended_artifact_fixes: Attach a formulas worksheet or CSV showing formula text for all derived cells.
Ensure expert memo contains explicit cell-level citations (Sheet!Cell) for all values reported.
verifier_issues_summary: Inconsistent pass/score fields across outputs (deterministic_precheck vs model grader).
Grader downgraded semantic score due to unverifiable spreadsheet formulas; memo-derived numbers were used as fallback.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce 235-revised that (1) includes either a parseable spreadsheet or a dedicated formulas-export worksheet/CSV showing all formulas for derived cells, (2) updates expert_memo.md to include explicit sheet+cell citations for every evidence and sensitivity value, (3) canonicalizes score/passed fields in the submission metadata, and (4) clearly states which claims are memo-derived vs spreadsheet-verified.
trace_specific_comments: Trace shows fallback OOXML writer and attempts to work around missing libraries; please surface a formula-export to make derived calculations auditable.
Trace and verifier note inconsistent score/passed fields between deterministic precheck and model grader outputs; canonicalize to avoid confusion.
